questions.q: By what honorific title is the Hindi film actress Meena Kumari most popularly remembered?
questions.explanation: Meena Kumari is widely remembered as 'The Tragedy Queen' of Hindi cinema for her many tragic roles.

questions.q: In which year did the Hindi film actress Meena Kumari pass away?
questions.explanation: Meena Kumari died in 1972, shortly after the release of her film Pakeezah.

questions.q: Which 1972 film, released just weeks before Meena Kumari's death, is considered one of her most celebrated works?
questions.explanation: Pakeezah, directed by Kamal Amrohi, was released in 1972 just weeks before Meena Kumari's death.

questions.q: Meena Kumari was a noted actress in which Indian film industry?
questions.explanation: Meena Kumari was a leading actress of Hindi cinema (Bollywood).

questions.q: Besides acting, Meena Kumari was also accomplished in which artistic field?
questions.explanation: Meena Kumari was an accomplished Urdu poet who wrote under the pen name 'Naaz'.

questions.q: In which 1962 Guru Dutt production did Meena Kumari play the tragic, alcoholic noblewoman Chhoti Bahu?
questions.explanation: Meena Kumari played Chhoti Bahu in the 1962 film Sahib Bibi Aur Ghulam.

questions.q: How many times did Meena Kumari win the Filmfare Award for Best Actress during her career?
questions.explanation: Meena Kumari won the Filmfare Best Actress award three times.

questions.q: Meena Kumari was born and raised primarily in which Indian city, the heart of the Hindi film industry?
questions.explanation: Meena Kumari was born in Bombay (now Mumbai) in 1933 and built her career there.

questions.q: In Pakeezah (1972), Meena Kumari played a character who was a member of which profession?
questions.explanation: In Pakeezah, Meena Kumari played Sahibjaan, a courtesan in Muslim Lucknow society.

questions.q: Which director, who was also married to Meena Kumari, made the film Pakeezah?
questions.explanation: Pakeezah was directed by Kamal Amrohi, who was married to Meena Kumari.

questions.q: In which year was the Hindi film actress Meena Kumari born?
questions.explanation: Meena Kumari was born on 1 August 1933 in Bombay.

questions.q: What was the birth name of the actress who later became known as Meena Kumari?
questions.explanation: Meena Kumari was born Mahjabeen Bano before adopting her screen name.

questions.q: At the 1963 Filmfare Awards, Meena Kumari achieved a unique distinction in the Best Actress category. What was it?
options: She was nominated for all three Best Actress slots
She was the first woman to win it
She tied for the award with another actress
questions.explanation: In 1963 Meena Kumari was famously nominated in all three Best Actress slots, an unmatched feat.

questions.q: For her role in Sahib Bibi Aur Ghulam (1962), Meena Kumari won which major award?
options: The National Film Award for Best Actress
The Filmfare Award for Best Actress
questions.explanation: Meena Kumari won the Filmfare Best Actress award for her performance as Chhoti Bahu in Sahib Bibi Aur Ghulam.

questions.q: Pakeezah (1972) had a famously long production history, taking roughly how long to complete from start to release?
questions.explanation: Pakeezah took about fourteen years to complete, delayed in part by the estrangement between Meena Kumari and Kamal Amrohi.

questions.q: Which composer created the celebrated music for Meena Kumari's film Pakeezah (1972)?
questions.explanation: The Pakeezah soundtrack was composed mainly by Ghulam Mohammed, with Naushad completing the background score after his death.

questions.q: The famous song 'Chalte Chalte Yunhi Koi Mil Gaya Tha' is from which Meena Kumari film?
questions.explanation: 'Chalte Chalte' is one of the best-loved songs from Pakeezah, picturized on Meena Kumari.

questions.q: In which 1952 musical film, directed by Vijay Bhatt, did Meena Kumari star opposite Bharat Bhushan in the title singer-composer's love story?
questions.explanation: Meena Kumari starred opposite Bharat Bhushan in Baiju Bawra (1952), a turning point that won her the first Filmfare Best Actress award.

questions.q: Meena Kumari won the very first Filmfare Award for Best Actress, presented in 1954, for her role in which film?
questions.explanation: Meena Kumari won the inaugural Filmfare Best Actress award for Baiju Bawra at the 1954 ceremony.

questions.q: In Bimal Roy's 1953 adaptation of Sarat Chandra Chattopadhyay's novel Parineeta, Meena Kumari played the lead role of which character?
questions.explanation: Meena Kumari played Lalita in Bimal Roy's 1953 film Parineeta, opposite Ashok Kumar.

questions.q: Meena Kumari's third and final Filmfare Best Actress win came for which 1966 film co-starring Raaj Kumar?
questions.explanation: Meena Kumari won her third Filmfare Best Actress award for the 1965 film Kaajal, presented in 1966.

questions.q: Under which pen name (takhallus) did Meena Kumari write her Urdu poetry?
questions.explanation: Meena Kumari wrote Urdu poetry under the pen name 'Naaz'.

questions.q: A posthumous album of Meena Kumari reciting her own poetry was released, produced with the help of which lyricist-poet?
questions.explanation: The album 'I Write, I Recite' featuring Meena Kumari's poetry was brought out with the help of Gulzar.

questions.q: As a child artiste in the early 1940s, Meena Kumari was often credited under which name?
questions.explanation: In her child-actor years Meena Kumari appeared in films credited as 'Baby Meena'.

questions.q: In which 1971 film, the directorial debut of Gulzar, did Meena Kumari play an elderly widow named Anandi caught between two rival youth gangs?
questions.explanation: Meena Kumari played the elderly widow Anandi in Mere Apne (1971), Gulzar's debut as a director.

questions.q: Meena Kumari made her film debut as a child performer in the 1939 film Leatherface, directed by which filmmaker?
questions.explanation: Meena Kumari began as a child actress under Vijay Bhatt, debuting in the 1939 film Leatherface.

questions.q: Which actor played the male lead Salim opposite Meena Kumari's Sahibjaan in the film Pakeezah (1972)?
questions.explanation: Raaj Kumar played the male lead Salim opposite Meena Kumari in Pakeezah.

questions.q: Meena Kumari's death in 1972 was primarily attributed to which medical condition, linked to years of alcohol use?
questions.explanation: Meena Kumari died of liver cirrhosis on 31 March 1972, a condition associated with prolonged alcohol use.

questions.q: In Sahib Bibi Aur Ghulam (1962), which actor played Bhoothnath, the servant through whose eyes Meena Kumari's Chhoti Bahu is seen?
questions.explanation: Guru Dutt played Bhoothnath in Sahib Bibi Aur Ghulam, while also producing the film.

questions.q: In her final years, Meena Kumari took an unusual step regarding her acclaimed performance in Pakeezah's production. What did she reportedly do?
options: She directed several of its scenes herself
She refused to dub her own dialogues
She worked on the film for a nominal fee of one rupee to help complete it
She insisted the film be shot abroad
questions.explanation: Despite their estrangement, Meena Kumari reportedly agreed to complete Pakeezah for a token fee of one rupee so the long-delayed film could be finished.
